rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BONHACH VS. MALIBU PIER PARTNERS LLC A DELAWARE PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 17, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY BONHACH MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Triable issue of fact raised regarding the nature of contract, and whether Bonhach was an at will employee or independent contractor. The language of the 6/18/04 email is ambiguous, referencing an "at will" relationship and then refers to plaintiff as a consultant. Both parties' depositions support Bonhach discretion to conduct himself similar to an independent contractor (Bonhach depos pp. 103, 118; Leff depo p. 138). Triable issues of fact whether contract breached and whether damages sustained. Triable issue of fact as to when exactly the breach occurred. Summary adjudication denied as to all separate "issues." Plaintiff fails to show how the evidence completely disposes of a simple cause of action or issue of duty. (CCP section 437c(f)(1); CRC 3.1112, 3.1113. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TRINA THORSEN VS. SHERIFF'S DEPARTMENT, CITY AND COUNTY OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 17, 2009, line 4, PLAINTIFF TRINA THORSEN MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Motion granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN KAZARYAN VS. ALEX SHAPIRO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 17, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT ALEX SHAPIRO, MARGARITA SHAPIRO, and, LEV SHAPIRO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendants shift the burden of proof and plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Defendants did not owe plaintiff a duty as a matter of law. No special relationship existed between plaintiff and defendants. The harm to plaintiff was not reasonably foreseeable. Yagian was not invited to the party and no copy of My Space Page provided. (See Margaret W. vs. Kelly R. (2006) 139 Cal.App. 4th 141.) The party did not constitute a dangerous condition of property and it was not foreseeable that Yagain would appear at the party, or that he would have an intention to assault. (See Eric J. vs. Betty M. (1999) 76 Cal.App. 4th 715.) No evidence that defendants ever communicated to plaintiff to support the negligent misrepresentation cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AMY YUE YI LUO VS. JODI ADAMS DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 17, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT JANET FRANKLIN RN, MICHELLE VASQUEZ RN, and, CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike off calendar as moot in view of ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AMY YUE YI LUO VS. JODI ADAMS DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 17, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT JANET FRANKLIN RN, MICHELLE VASQUEZ RN, and, CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Complaint is time-barred. Plaintiff was told on December 20, 2007 that she "may have nerve or tendon damage." On January 3, 2008 Dr. Kao upon examining plaintiff's hand stated "why did you wait so long, you need surgery." These events would put a reasonable person on inquiry and would arouse suspicion. See Knowles vs. Superior Court (2004) 118 Cal.App. 4th 1290. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al VS. SIDNEY FERNALD DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 17, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT INNOVATIVE LEASE SERVICES, INC. Motion To Set Aside Default. Motion to set aside default granted pursuant to CCP section 473(b). Mistake, inadvertance and excusable neglect demonstrated by office move and personal emergencies. Defendant moved quickly to have default set aside. Request for CCP section 473(c)(1) penalty denied as defendant was forced to bring this motion after plaintiff refused to voluntarily set aside the default. =(302/CWW)
